# google_dialogflow_sip_trunk

SipTrunk is the resource that represents a SIP trunk to connect to the Google Telephony Platform SIP trunking service.


To get more information about SipTrunk, see:

* [API documentation](https://cloud.google.com/dialogflow/es/docs/reference/rest/v2/projects.locations.sipTrunks)

<div class = "oics-button" style="float: right; margin: 0 0 -15px">

    <img alt="Open in Cloud Shell" src="//gstatic.com/cloudssh/images/open-btn.svg" style="max-height: 44px; margin: 32px auto; max-width: 100%;">
  </a>
</div>
## Example Usage - Dialogflow Sip Trunk Basic


```hcl
resource "google_dialogflow_sip_trunk" "basic_trunk" {
  display_name = "basic-trunk"
  location     = "europe-west3"
  expected_hostname = [
    "basic-trunk.example.com"
  ]
}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


* `expected_hostname` -
  (Required)
  Required. The expected hostnames in the peer certificate from the partner that is used for TLS authentication.

* `location` -
  (Required)
  The location of the SIP trunk.


* `display_name` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Human-readable alias for this trunk.

* `project` - (Optional) The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
    If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

* `deletion_policy` - (Optional) Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the resource. Defaults to DELETE.
	When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the resource,
	the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
	When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
	management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
	When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.


## Attributes Reference

In addition to the arguments listed above, the following computed attributes are exported:

* `id` - an identifier for the resource with format `{{name}}`

* `name` -
  Identifier. The unique identifier of the SIP trunk.

* `connections` -
  Output only. The connections of the SIP trunk.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_connections).


<a name="nested_connections"></a>The `connections` block contains:

* `connection_id` -
  (Output)
  Output only. The unique identifier of the connection.

* `state` -
  (Output)
  Output only. The state of the connection.

* `update_time` -
  (Output)
  Output only. The last update time of the connection.

* `error_details` -
  (Output)
  Output only. The error details of the connection.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_connections_error_details).


<a name="nested_connections_error_details"></a>The `error_details` block contains:

* `code` -
  (Output)
  Output only. The error code.

* `message` -
  (Output)
  Output only. The error message.

## Timeouts

This resource provides the following
[Timeouts](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/sdkv2/resources/retries-and-customizable-timeouts) configuration options:

- `create` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `update` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `delete` - Default is 20 minutes.

## Import


SipTrunk can be imported using any of these accepted formats:

* `{{name}}`

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, use an [`identity` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/block/import#identity) to import SipTrunk using identity values. For example:

```tf
import {
  identity = {
    name = "<-optional value->"
  }
  to = google_dialogflow_sip_trunk.default
}
```

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import SipTrunk using one of the formats above. For example:

```tf
import {
  id = "{{name}}"
  to = google_dialogflow_sip_trunk.default
}
```

When using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import), SipTrunk can be imported using one of the formats above. For example:

```
$ terraform import google_dialogflow_sip_trunk.default {{name}}
```

## User Project Overrides

This resource supports [User Project Overrides](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google/latest/docs/guides/provider_reference#user_project_override).
